# Gephi analysis guidelines — Pharmaceutical trade network

This document is the **reference for the Gephi-based network analysis** of the DM II project. It lists the research questions, links them to concrete steps in Gephi, and specifies which data and metrics to use. Use it to drive the analysis and to structure the poster/report.

**Data:** All files are in `Outputs/gephi/`, generated by `Python files/02_prepare_gephi.py`. Run that script once to produce every graph variant below.

---

## Data and graph variants (all produced by `02_prepare_gephi.py`)

### Full and temporal (Sections 1, 2, 4)

| Use | Files | Description |
|-----|--------|-------------|
| **Full graph (all years, all products)** | `nodes.csv`, `edges.csv` or `nodes_all.csv`, `edges_all.csv` | Country–country, 2016–2024, directed, weight = trade value (thousand USD). Default for structure and sensitivity. |
| **Single year** | `nodes_YYYY.csv`, `edges_YYYY.csv` | Same graph restricted to year YYYY (2016 … 2024). For year-by-year evolution. |
| **Pre-COVID period** | `nodes_2016_2019.csv`, `edges_2016_2019.csv` | Flows in 2016–2019. |
| **COVID / post-COVID period** | `nodes_2020_2024.csv`, `edges_2020_2024.csv` | Flows in 2020–2024. |

### Product-level (Section 3)

| Use | Files | Description |
|-----|--------|-------------|
| **Vaccines only (HS 3002)** | `nodes_vaccines.csv`, `edges_vaccines.csv` | Country–country flows for vaccine products (HS 300210–300290), all years. |
| **Vaccines by period** | `nodes_vaccines_2016_2019.csv`, `edges_vaccines_2016_2019.csv` and `nodes_vaccines_2020_2024.csv`, `edges_vaccines_2020_2024.csv` | Vaccine trade in pre-COVID vs COVID/post-COVID. |
| **Other pharma (HS 30 excluding vaccines)** | `nodes_other_pharma.csv`, `edges_other_pharma.csv` | Country–country flows for all other HS 30 products, all years. |
| **Other pharma by period** | `nodes_other_pharma_2016_2019.csv`, `edges_other_pharma_2016_2019.csv` and `nodes_other_pharma_2020_2024.csv`, `edges_other_pharma_2020_2024.csv` | Other pharma in pre-COVID vs COVID/post-COVID. |

**Product definition:** Vaccines = HS 6-digit codes 300210–300290 (human and animal vaccines, blood fractions, toxins/cultures). Other pharma = remaining HS 30 (300110–300199, 300310–300660).

---

## How to use these files in Gephi (step by step)

You always work with **one graph at a time**: one pair of node + edge files (e.g. `nodes.csv` + `edges.csv` for the full network). Repeat the steps below for each graph you want to analyse (full, single year, period, vaccines, etc.).

### 1. Open Gephi and create a new project

- **File → New Project** (or open an existing one).
- You will import data via the **Data Laboratory**, not “Open”.

### 2. Import the **nodes** table

- Go to **Data Laboratory** (tab at the top).
- Click **Import Spreadsheet**.
- Choose the **nodes** file (e.g. `Outputs/gephi/nodes.csv`).
- In the import wizard:
  - **Separator:** Comma.
  - **Charset:** UTF-8 (or as needed if labels look wrong).
  - **Import as:** **Nodes table**.
  - Ensure **Id** is set as the node ID column and **Label** as the label column (Gephi often detects these).
- Click **Finish**. You should see the list of nodes (countries) in the Data Laboratory.

### 3. Import the **edges** table

- Still in **Data Laboratory**, click **Import Spreadsheet** again.
- Choose the **edges** file (e.g. `Outputs/gephi/edges.csv`).
- In the wizard:
  - **Separator:** Comma.
  - **Import as:** **Edges table**.
  - Set **Source** = column that contains the exporter country code (Source).
  - Set **Target** = column that contains the importer country code (Target).
  - **Weight** = the column with trade value (Weight). If Gephi asks, confirm it is a numeric edge attribute.
  - If there is a **Type** column, you can map it; our edges are **Directed**.
- Click **Finish**. Edges are merged with the existing nodes. If you see “Create missing nodes”, you can leave it unchecked since we already imported nodes.

### 4. Set the graph as directed and use edge weight

- Switch to the **Overview** tab (the graph view).
- The graph is directed by default if the edges table had a Type = Directed. If not: **Graph → Summary** and check that the graph is **Directed**.
- Edge **Weight** is used automatically for “Weighted Degree” and other weighted statistics; no extra setting needed if you mapped the Weight column.

### 5. Run statistics

- Open the **Statistics** panel (right side, or **Window → Statistics**).
- Run (in any order, but run each before using it for layout/partition):
  - **Degree** — gives In-Degree and Out-Degree (number of partners).
  - **Weighted Degree** — gives strength (sum of trade value) per node.
  - **Betweenness Centrality** — for directed graph.
  - **PageRank** (or **Eigenvector Centrality**).
- Click **Run** for each; accept the default options if unsure. Results appear as new **node columns** in the Data Laboratory and can be used for **size** and **color** in the Overview.

### 6. Apply a layout

- In the **Layout** panel (left side), choose e.g. **Force Atlas 2**.
- Optional: check **Prevent Overlap**, or use **LinLog** for clearer separation of clusters.
- Click **Run** and wait until the layout stabilises (you can stop it when it looks good).
- You can **size nodes** by a metric (e.g. Weighted Degree or Betweenness): in **Appearance → Nodes → Size**, choose **Ranking** and select the metric.

### 7. Detect communities (optional)

- In **Statistics**, run **Modularity**.
- Choose a **Resolution** (e.g. 1.0); click **OK** and **Run**.
- In **Appearance → Nodes → Partition**, select the new **Modularity Class** (or the partition name Gephi gave). Nodes are colored by community.

### 8. Export for the poster or report

- **Preview** tab: adjust labels, node size, edge thickness, then **Export to SVG/PDF/PNG** for high-resolution figures.
- **Data Laboratory**: you can **Export table** to CSV to get a spreadsheet of nodes with all computed metrics (degree, strength, betweenness, PageRank, modularity class) for tables in your report.

### Quick reference: which file pair for what

| What you want to do | Use these files |
|---------------------|-----------------|
| Overall structure, central countries, sensitivity | `nodes.csv`, `edges.csv` (or `nodes_all.csv`, `edges_all.csv`) |
| One specific year | `nodes_YYYY.csv`, `edges_YYYY.csv` |
| Pre-COVID vs COVID/post-COVID | `nodes_2016_2019.csv` + `edges_2016_2019.csv` and `nodes_2020_2024.csv` + `edges_2020_2024.csv` |
| Vaccines only (all years or by period) | `nodes_vaccines.csv` + `edges_vaccines.csv` (and `_2016_2019`, `_2020_2024` if needed) |
| Other pharma only | `nodes_other_pharma.csv` + `edges_other_pharma.csv` (and period variants) |

---

## 1. Spatial / network structure (countries)

**Goal:** Identify which countries are central and influential, whether there are clusters or regional blocs, and how major economic/geographic blocs differ in position and connectivity.

### Research questions

- Which countries occupy the **most central and influential** positions in the pharmaceutical trade network (e.g. degree, strength, centrality)?
- Can we identify **clusters or communities** of countries that trade more intensely with each other (e.g. regional blocs, specialization patterns)?
- How do **major economic or geographic blocs** (EU, North America, Asia, South America) differ in terms of network position and connectivity?

### Gephi steps and metrics

1. **Import:** Load the country–country graph (nodes then edges). Set the graph as **directed** and use **Weight** = trade value.
2. **Statistics (Statistics panel):**
   - **Degree** (in/out): number of trade partners (export/import links). Use for “connectivity” and hub identification.
   - **Weighted Degree (Strength):** sum of edge weights (total export/import value). Use for “volume” and economic importance.
   - **Betweenness Centrality:** mediation role; countries on many shortest paths are brokers.
   - **PageRank (or Eigenvector Centrality):** influence considering the importance of partners. Good for “influence” beyond raw degree/strength.
3. **Layout:** e.g. **Force Atlas 2** (or Yifan Hu) with:
   - Repulsion and attraction by degree/weight so that central nodes are clearly visible.
   - Optionally use **LinLog** or **No Overlap** for readability.
4. **Communities:** Run **Modularity** (resolution as needed). Color nodes by partition to see **clusters** (regional or trade blocs).
5. **Bloc comparison:** Add a node attribute for **region/bloc** (EU, North America, Asia, South America, etc.). In Gephi: duplicate the graph or use filters; then compare average degree, strength, betweenness, and PageRank **by bloc** (e.g. via Data Laboratory export + Excel/Python, or filters + statistics).

### What to report

- **Rankings:** Top 10–15 countries by out-degree, in-degree, out-strength, in-strength, betweenness, PageRank.
- **Communities:** Number and composition of modules; whether they align with geography (e.g. EU cluster, Americas, Asia).
- **Bloc comparison:** Table or figure comparing connectivity and centrality by bloc; short interpretation (e.g. “EU and North America show higher betweenness; Asia shows …”).

---

## 2. Temporal dynamics (evolution of the network)

**Goal:** Describe how the network structure changes over time and whether structural breaks or shifts appear around or after the COVID-19 period.

### Research questions

- How does the **structure** of the pharmaceutical trade network **change over time** (e.g. emergence of new hubs, changes in centrality or connectivity)?
- What **structural breaks or shifts** can be observed around/after the **COVID-19** period?

### Gephi steps and metrics

1. **Data:** Use the period and single-year files produced by `02_prepare_gephi.py`:
   - **Periods:** `nodes_2016_2019.csv` / `edges_2016_2019.csv` (pre-COVID) and `nodes_2020_2024.csv` / `edges_2020_2024.csv` (COVID/post-COVID).
   - **Single years:** `nodes_YYYY.csv` / `edges_YYYY.csv` for YYYY = 2016 … 2024 (e.g. 2019, 2020, 2021 for around COVID-19).
2. **For each period/year:** Import the corresponding nodes and edges, then run the same set of metrics (degree, weighted degree, betweenness, PageRank) and the same layout and community detection (e.g. Modularity with same resolution).
3. **Comparison:**
   - **Rankings over time:** Top central countries in each period; note newcomers or dropouts.
   - **Stability of communities:** Do module memberships change? Do new clusters appear (e.g. vaccine-producing hubs)?
   - **Global metrics:** Density, average degree, total weight; compare pre-COVID vs during/post-COVID.
4. **Visualization:** Side-by-side or small multiples of the same layout/metrics for 2016–2019 vs 2020–2024 (or key years). Use the same node set and, if possible, similar layout parameters for comparability.

### What to report

- **Evolution:** Table or time series of top central countries and of global metrics (density, total edges, total weight) by year or period.
- **COVID-19:** Narrative on structural breaks: e.g. changes in hub rankings, new communities, or shifts in connectivity; link to vaccines/supply chain if supported by the product-level analysis (Section 3).

---

## 3. Product-level (e.g. vaccines vs other pharma)

**Goal:** Understand which products or product categories are most central, whether country–product trade profiles form communities, and how the importance of specific groups (e.g. vaccines) changes over time, including around COVID-19.

### Research questions

- Which **products or product categories** (e.g. vaccines vs other pharmaceuticals) are **most central** in the country–product trade network?
- Can we identify **communities of countries** linked by **similar product trade profiles** (e.g. exporters of high-value patented products vs importers of generics)?
- How does the **importance of specific product groups** (e.g. vaccines) in the network **change over time**, particularly around COVID-19?

### Gephi steps and metrics

1. **Graphs to use (all in `Outputs/gephi/`):**
   - **Vaccines:** `nodes_vaccines.csv` / `edges_vaccines.csv` (all years); `nodes_vaccines_2016_2019.csv` / `edges_vaccines_2016_2019.csv` and `nodes_vaccines_2020_2024.csv` / `edges_vaccines_2020_2024.csv` (by period).
   - **Other pharma:** `nodes_other_pharma.csv` / `edges_other_pharma.csv` (all years); same period suffixes `_2016_2019` and `_2020_2024`.
   - Each graph is **country–country**, directed, weighted by trade value for that product group (and time window).
2. **Metrics:** Same as Section 1: degree (in/out), weighted degree, betweenness, PageRank, Modularity. Run on each product graph and compare.
3. **Vaccines vs other pharma:** Compare centrality rankings and community structure between `edges_vaccines.csv` and `edges_other_pharma.csv`; then compare vaccines (and other pharma) **pre-COVID vs COVID/post-COVID** using the `_2016_2019` and `_2020_2024` files.
4. **Profiles and communities:** Color or partition by export profile; check whether Modularity clusters align with product specialization (e.g. vaccine hubs vs generics importers).

### What to report

- **Centrality by product group:** Which countries are most central in vaccine trade vs other pharma; how rankings differ.
- **Communities:** Whether clusters reflect product profiles (e.g. vaccine hubs, generics importers).
- **Time:** How vaccine (and other key product) centrality and community structure change around 2020–2021; brief link to COVID-19 and supply chain narrative.

---

## 4. Sensitivity analysis (excluding top countries)

**Goal:** Check whether main conclusions about structure and evolution are robust when the most central or highest-volume countries are removed.

### Research questions

- How do **main network patterns** (centrality rankings, clusters, etc.) **change** when we **exclude the most central or high-volume** countries from the analysis?
- Are **key conclusions** about structure and evolution **robust** when top exporters/importers are removed?

### Gephi steps and metrics

1. **Baseline:** Run full analysis (Section 1) on the full graph — note top exporters/importers by degree and strength, and main communities.
2. **Filtered graphs:** In Python (or Gephi), create versions of the graph where you **remove**:
   - e.g. top 5 or top 10 **exporters** by out-strength,
   - or top 5 or top 10 **importers** by in-strength,
   - or top 5 **by betweenness** (global brokers).
   Export new nodes/edges (only nodes that remain and edges between them) and re-import into Gephi.
3. **Re-run:** On each filtered graph, recompute degree, strength, betweenness, PageRank, and Modularity (same parameters as baseline).
4. **Compare:**
   - **Rankings:** Do the next-tier countries move up in a consistent way? Do new “hubs” appear?
   - **Communities:** Do cluster memberships and number of modules change strongly?
   - **Global metrics:** Density, average degree; how much do they drop?

### What to report

- **Robustness:** Short summary: “Conclusions on [X] hold / do not hold when top N countries are excluded.”
- **Sensitivity:** Which metrics or clusters are most affected (e.g. betweenness vs degree); one small table or figure comparing full vs filtered is enough.

### Optional extension

Repeat the same sensitivity for **single-year or period** graphs (e.g. 2020–2024) to check whether temporal conclusions (Section 2) are also robust to excluding top countries.

---

## Practical checklist (Gephi workflow)

- [ ] Import nodes then edges; set **directed** and **Weight**.
- [ ] Run **Statistics:** Degree (in/out), Weighted Degree, Betweenness, PageRank (and optionally Eigenvector).
- [ ] Run **Modularity** (partition); apply partition to **node color**.
- [ ] Apply **layout** (e.g. Force Atlas 2); optionally size/color nodes by a metric.
- [ ] Add **region/bloc** attribute for bloc comparison (Section 1).
- [ ] Export **graph and statistics** for each variant: full graph, single years or periods (Section 2), product subsets (Section 3), filtered top countries (Section 4).
- [ ] For **poster/report:** Export high-resolution PNG/PDF of key layouts; export Data Laboratory table (node Id, Label, metrics, partition) for tables and robustness discussion.

---

## Summary table: questions → metrics and outputs

| Section | Main questions | Key Gephi metrics | Main outputs |
|--------|-----------------|--------------------|-------------|
| **1. Spatial** | Central countries, communities, bloc differences | Degree, Weighted degree, Betweenness, PageRank, Modularity | Rankings, partition map, bloc comparison table |
| **2. Temporal** | Evolution, COVID-19 structural break | Same as 1, per period | Period comparison, time series of rankings/metrics |
| **3. Product** | Central products, product-based communities, vaccines over time | Same on product-filtered or bipartite graphs | Product-group comparison, vaccine vs other, time comparison |
| **4. Sensitivity** | Robustness without top countries | Same on filtered graph | Full vs filtered comparison, robustness statement |

All graph variants (full, temporal by year and period, product-level vaccines and other pharma by period) are produced by `02_prepare_gephi.py`; no extra scripting is required. Use this document as the base for the Gephi analysis and to structure the poster.
